ground_truth.title: Everything You Need to Know About Online Tutoring
ground_truth.author: Sasha Blakeley
ground_truth.main_content: Recent years have seen a boom in the need for and prevalence of online learning systems for students of all ages. With this major change in education, many students have been turning to online tutoring to get extra support with their schoolwork. Working with a tutor can be helpful for a variety of reasons, and tutoring, whether online or in person, is something that many students may want to consider at some point in their academic careers. An online tutor, sometimes called a virtual tutor, works with a student over an online video chat. This can be efficient for both student and tutor, cutting down on travel time and providing an accessible and COVID-safe option for educational help. A tutor's main role is one of support. They work with a student's school material to fill in gaps, review material, and help explain and practice concepts that students might be struggling with. Tutoring is by no means equivalent to or a replacement for in-class education, but it can complement that education and give students a more complete academic understanding. Online tutoring can work in a variety of ways depending on the tutoring service, the age of the student, and the student's needs. Subject-based tutoring helps students struggling with specific subjects like math or science. Holistic tutoring helps students develop broadly applicable academic skills like note-taking and organization. Test preparation tutoring helps students prepare for exams like the SAT or ACT. The most important element of any online tutoring setup is a good internet connection since online tutoring takes place primarily over video chat. Finding the right online tutor requires patience and thorough research. A good online tutor should have traits like adaptability, patience, good listening skills, clear communication, approachability, expertise, organization, and reliability. Tutoring costs typically range from $10 to $100 per hour depending on the tutor's experience and the type of tutoring needed. Online tutoring provides flexibility and personalized attention that can make all the difference when it comes to academics.
